issue: Theoretical contribution unclear — reads as loss aversion repackaged
suggested_fix: Add 2 paragraphs distinguishing option-level loss avoidance from prospect theory outcome-level loss aversion. Introduce loss-attention framing with testable predictions.

issue: Only 8 references — unacceptable for journal paper in mature field
suggested_fix: Add 15-20 references: priority heuristic, regret theory, description-experience gap, instance-based learning, probability weighting, salience theory, configural weight models.

issue: No inferential statistics for key claims
suggested_fix: Add bootstrap CIs for AUC values. Add proportion test or chi-square for 38% vs 25% failure rate difference. Add CI for cross-transfer AUC.

issue: Circularity — graph built from same features as regression
suggested_fix: Either add permutation test for 1.9x density ratio or demote graph to supplementary material with 1 paragraph in main text.

issue: Dominance 'cognitive competence' may be confounded with problem easiness
suggested_fix: Check whether dominance is confounded with ev_abs_diff. If yes soften claim. If no state explicitly that dominance adds beyond EV difference.
Add paragraph: data publicly available at [URLs]. Analysis code available at [GitHub/OSF].

issue: Interpretive causal language despite block/feedback confound
suggested_fix: Rename to 'Changes Across Blocks'. Replace 'learning' with 'improvement'. Reduce causal framing.
Rewrite first sentence as question: 'Why do people reject options that maximize expected value?'
Add paragraph discussing whether CPT with standard parameters predicts the same pattern. Note that binary presence vs probability-weighted magnitude is the key distinction.
Give anti-learning more prominence — it's the most novel finding. Consider a dedicated subsection.
Move to supplementary. Keep 1 paragraph + Figure 5 in main text. Remove Figure 6 from main text.
Replace with aggregate statistic: fraction of cross-dataset neighbors sharing EV-failure status vs random baseline.
Remove or move to supplementary materials.
Open with a concrete example problem showing loss-driven EV failure before the theoretical framing.
Add summary figure of feature groups or move full feature list to appendix.
Add 2-3 sentences on decision support applications.
